qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Repeated wildcard glob patterns trigger catastrophic regex backtracking
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same vulnerable logic and root cause as GHSA-3ppc-4f35-3m26: consecutive non-globstar `*` characters are compiled in `src/ast.ts` into separate `[^/]*?` regex groups instead of being coalesced, so a later missing literal forces exponential backtracking until the 2e111f3 patch.

selected_finding.title: Repeated wildcard glob patterns trigger catastrophic regex backtracking

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding matches the advisory's root cause in minimatch's repeated-wildcard regex compilation path, and the copied PoC reproduced the advisory-described denial-of-service consequence with a single failing match that consumed about 1.98 seconds of CPU time.
evidence: The advisory summary and patch 2e111f3a79abc00fa73110195de2c0f2351904f5 describe coalescing consecutive non-globstar `*` characters in `src/ast.ts`, which is the same vulnerable operation named in `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_5fc4c6f5/repeated_wildcards_redos/vulnerability_report.json`.

The fresh copied PoC run in `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_5fc4c6f5/.judge/poc_runs/repeated_wildcards_redos/run.log` produced a regex with repeated `[^/]*?` groups followed by a missing literal `X`, demonstrating activation of the advisory-described vulnerable compilation path.

The runtime artifact `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_5fc4c6f5/.judge/poc_runs/repeated_wildcards_redos/observed_result.json` recorded `result=false` and `elapsedMs=1984.459666` for one `minimatch()` call, which is a concrete availability impact caused by the advisory root cause.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The adapted copied PoC installed `minimatch@10.2.0`, generated a regex containing repeated `[^/]*?` groups for pattern `***************X***`, returned `false` on a non-matching string, and spent 1984.459666 ms in a single `minimatch()` call.
